hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output value must strictly follow the 'YYYY-1-M-Day' structure (e.g., 2019-1-2), ensuring single-digit months and days are single characters with no padding.
When the input integer is exactly 9007199254740993, the system must return the exact error line: 'error=integer_out_of_bounds' followed by 'value=9007199254740993'.
For array inputs with an explicit type list, values must be serialized as an object with key 'value' and key 'arrayValues' containing the raw string representation of the elements.

context_pointers.vague_reference: conforms to standard array type inference rules used elsewhere
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output 'arrayType' field must exactly match the inner type string (e.g., 'string', 'number', 'null') identified within the input array.

context_pointers.vague_reference: maps nested structure definitions to their deep types
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The 'structTypes' array must contain an object where keys are the property names from the input object and values are the canonical type strings (e.g., 'string', 'integer', 'bool').

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_endpoint_normalization.json.description: Endpoint normalization adds a secure protocol when missing, preserves an explicit protocol, and removes trailing slash characters.

feature2_1_temporal_literals.json.description: Temporal literal wrappers preserve date, datetime, time, and timestamp inputs in the wire value shape used for query parameters and row data.

feature2_2_geography_integer_literals.js.description: Geography and integer literal wrappers preserve their wire values, including JSON representation and integer value conversion behavior.

feature3_1_parameter_type_inference.json.description: Query parameter type inference maps primitive, custom, array, and structured values into the expected query parameter type descriptors and reports neutral errors when a type cannot be inferred.

feature3_2_provided_parameter_types.json.description: Explicit parameter type declarations for arrays and structures are converted into query parameter descriptors, while invalid declarations produce a neutral error category.

feature3_3_query_parameter_serialization.description: Query parameter serialization combines inferred or provided type descriptors with values, including nested structures, arrays, and typed literals.

feature4_schema_row_merge.json.description: Schema-guided row decoding converts API row cells into named objects, preserving nested records and repeated fields with domain type conversions.

feature5_1_schema_string_parsing.json.description: Comma-separated schema strings are parsed into field definitions with trimmed names and uppercased types.

feature5_2_table_metadata_formatting.jso.description: Table metadata formatting converts friendly names, schema shortcuts, partitioning shortcuts, and view SQL into the API request body shape.

feature5_3_insert_value_encoding.json.description: Inserted row values are recursively encoded so typed literals become wire values while arrays and objects keep their original structure.

feature6_resource_references.json.description: Dataset and table reference factories retain identifiers and propagate default locations unless an explicit child location is supplied.

feature7_1_create_table_requests.json.description: Creating a table produces an API request with the table identifier, formatted metadata, and inherited location when available.

feature7_2_query_job_requests.json.description: Creating a query job produces a job configuration with standard SQL, parameter mode, serialized parameters, dry-run settings, labels, timeout, job id or prefix, destination tables, and location where supplied.

feature7_3_extract_job_requests.json.description: Extracting models or tables produces job configurations that identify the source resource, destination URIs, destination format, compression, job id or prefix, and location where applicable.

feature7_4_copy_job_requests.json.description: Copying table data produces a job configuration that points to source and destination table references and carries job-level options.

feature8_1_insert_requests.json.description: Inserting rows produces an insert-all API request that encodes row JSON, adds insert identifiers by default, supports raw rows, and reports empty input as a neutral error.

feature8_2_iam_requests.json.description: Table IAM operations produce policy and permission request bodies, and unsupported policy versions are reported as a neutral error category.

_constraints.output_rule: Return normalized endpoint string followed by a newline
Prefix with 'https://' (missing treated as secure)
Preserve the existing protocol exactly as provided
Remove all trailing slash characters
Format output 'value' with YYYY-1-M-Day structure if structured input provided

_constraints.input_condition: temporal kind is 'date' and string input provided
_constraints.output_rule: Format output 'value' to match input string exactly, no formatting applied
Output type 'geography_literal', value as is, json as is
Do not produce error output, imply success conversion
Return error line 'error=integer_out_of_bounds' and 'value=9007199254740993'
Infer type 'BOOL'
Infer type 'FLOAT64'

_constraints.input_condition: value is empty array or null
_constraints.output_rule: Produce 'missing-type' error output

_constraints.input_condition: provided type is array of strings
_constraints.output_rule: Output type 'ARRAY' with 'arrayType' containing the inner type

_constraints.input_condition: provided type is object with named props
_constraints.output_rule: Output type 'STRUCT' with 'structTypes' array mapping names to types

_constraints.input_condition: value is primitive/null/unsupported for empty detection
_constraints.output_rule: Standard serialization applies (errors handled elsewhere per test cases)

_constraints.input_condition: value is array with explicit type list
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'ARRAY' type with 'arrayType' matching the type list, serialize values as {value: X}

_constraints.input_condition: value is object with nested literals
_constraints.output_rule: Infer literal types (DATE, etc.) in the type structure, serialize literal value as string
Collapse repeated field values into a JSON array

_constraints.input_condition: schema field mode is not 'REPEATED' or 'RECORD'
_constraints.output_rule: Assign to single object property under field name
Maintain nested object structure under field name
Convert to boolean true/false

_constraints.input_condition: wrapIntegers option is true and value is '123'
_constraints.output_rule: While parse succeeds, if integer cannot be converted to integer type safely, return error
Output object with 'fields' array containing parsed name/type pairs
Convert 'integer' to type 'INTEGER'
Convert 'bool' to type 'BOOL'
Convert to type 'FLOAT'
Output 'friendlyName' key with the value

_constraints.input_condition: view query provided and useLegacySql is false/not provided
_constraints.output_rule: Default 'useLegacySql' to false in output
Replace literal object with the raw string value
Output 'null' alone
Output location as 'EU' from input, parentProjectId must be set
Output location based on datasetLocation input
Convert schema string to 'fields' array in JSON body
Omit 'friendlyName' from JSON body
Inject 'projectId' as 'project-id' if not explicitly provided
Include location context in tableReference (implied by context)
Set 'parameterMode' to 'named', include 'queryParameters' array
Set 'parameterMode' to 'positional', omit 'name' from queryParameter objects
Strip query body context when using explicit list modes
Output 'types' as array and 'parameterMode' as 'positional'
Return error 'invalid_destination_format'
Use provided sourceProjectId in sourceTable reference
Use provided destProjectId in destinationTable reference
Output 'location' as 'EU'

_constraints.input_condition: table_insert_request with typed literal value in rows object
_constraints.output_rule: Encode literal value as raw string in the row JSON
Preserve 'insertId' and raw structure exactly as provided in input

_constraints.input_condition: action is 'set' with policy and bindings
_constraints.output_rule: Include 'etag' at top level and 'policy' object in JSON body
Ensure 'version' key is present in policy object
